Module com.example.cincuentazo
Package com.example.cincuentazo.controller

Class StartController

java.lang.Object
com.example.cincuentazo.controller.StartController
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class StartController extends Object implements javafx.fxml.Initializable
Controller for the start view, where the human player chooses how many machine opponents (1, 2 or 3) to play against before starting the game.

This controller belongs to the controller layer of the MVC architecture. It reacts to user input on the start screen and, in later phases, will request the transition to the game view through the view manager.

    Description
    private javafx.scene.control.RadioButton
    oneOpponentRadio
    Radio button to play against one machine opponent.
    private javafx.scene.control.ToggleGroup
    opponentsToggleGroup
    Toggle group that ensures only one opponent option is selected.
    private javafx.scene.control.Button
    startButton
    Button that starts the game with the selected number of opponents.
    private javafx.scene.control.Label
    statusLabel
    Label used to display status or feedback messages to the player.
    private javafx.scene.control.RadioButton
    threeOpponentsRadio
    Radio button to play against three machine opponents.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL location, ResourceBundle resources)
      Initialization callback invoked automatically after the FXML view has been loaded. Groups the radio buttons together and wires the start button action.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable
      Parameters:
      location - the FXML location (provided by JavaFX, unused here)
      resources - the resource bundle (provided by JavaFX, unused here)

    • handleStartGame

      private void handleStartGame()
      Handles the start button action by requesting the game view, passing the selected number of machine opponents.

    • getSelectedOpponents

      private int getSelectedOpponents()
      Returns the number of machine opponents selected by the player.
      Returns:
      the number of opponents (1, 2 or 3)
